pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals website (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/8) lists 2.6%; NIOSH sources list 2.5%. Discrepancy exists between sources.
If using CAMEO value of 2.6%: 10% of 2.6% × 10,000 = 2600 ppm. If using NIOSH value of 2.5%: 10% of 2.5% × 10,000 = 2500 ppm

pi_items.metadata_value: 10% of LEL percent = setpoint percent; multiply by 10,000 to convert to ppm
pi_items.verified_value: 10% of LEL percent = setpoint percent; multiply by 10,000 to convert to ppm

privileged_info_summary: The gas alarm setpoint calculation task uses real data from CAMEO Chemicals (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/), a public NOAA chemical database. Metadata provides expected LEL values for four chemicals: Methane (5.0%), Propane (2.1%), Hydrogen (4.0%), and Acetone (2.5%). The first three LEL values are verified correct against CAMEO Chemicals and NIOSH sources. A discrepancy exists for Acetone: CAMEO Chemicals lists 2.6% while NIOSH sources list 2.5%; the task metadata follows the NIOSH value (2.5%). The conversion formula (10% of LEL in percent volume × 10,000 = ppm) is mathematically correct. All four expected PPM values are correctly calculated from their respective LEL values. The output file path (/home/ga/Documents/alarm_setpoints.txt) is correctly specified. The verifier's tolerances (±0.2% for LEL, ±200 ppm for setpoint) can accommodate both CAMEO and NIOSH acetone values, so this discrepancy is unlikely to cause task failures.
